product_categories.category_warning: Most mainstream cereals are NOT gluten-free. Malt flavoring (barley-derived) is the most common hidden source. Always look for an explicit certified GF label.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Contains malt flavoring derived from barley. NOT gluten-free despite appearing rice/corn based.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Erewhon Crispy Brown Rice, Three Wishes (certified GF)

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made with non-certified oats. Many formulations also contain wheat. Require explicit certified GF label.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Kind GF-labelled bars, RXBAR (check label)

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Cheerios uses a mechanical sorting process GFWD considers unreliable. Quaker Oats are not certified GF. High cross-contamination risk.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Certified purity-protocol oats only

product_categories.category_warning: One of the highest-risk categories. Soy sauce, malt vinegar, wheat flour thickeners, and hydrolyzed wheat protein hide in nearly every sauce type.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Fermented from wheat and soy. Wheat is typically listed first. NOT gluten-free.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Kikkoman GF soy sauce, San-J Tamari GF, Coconut Secret coconut aminos

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Based on soy sauce — inherits wheat content.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Certified GF teriyaki

examples: Lea & Perrins UK and Canada versions
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: UK and Canadian Lea & Perrins contains malt vinegar. US Lea & Perrins uses distilled vinegar and is GF.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Lea & Perrins (US), French's Worcestershire

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Traditionally made with wheat flour. Contains gluten.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Certified GF hoisin only

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Modified starch and caramel colour from wheat. Often contains soy sauce.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Certified GF oyster sauce

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat flour is the primary thickener in virtually all commercial gravy.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Certified GF gravy mixes only

product_categories.category_warning: Most beverages are safe. Beer and malt beverages are the primary risk.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Brewed from malted barley. Contains gluten.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Ground Breaker Brewing, Glutenberg, New Planet, Ghostfish Brewing

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from gluten-containing grains then treated. Standard tests cannot accurately measure residual gluten. NOT safe for celiac.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Dedicated GF beers brewed from sorghum, rice, or millet

examples: Any shake with 'malt' or 'malted' in name
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Malted milk powder contains dried whole milk, malted barley, and wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Request shake without malt powder

confirmed_safe_products: Cider (apple and pear — confirm no malt)

product_categories.category_warning: Flavoured snacks are high risk — seasonings frequently contain wheat, malt vinegar, or soy sauce. Plain varieties are usually safe.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Malt vinegar is the standard 'vinegar' flavour in UK and many US salt & vinegar snacks.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Lay's confirmed GF varieties, Cape Cod (check flavour)

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Snyder's GF pretzels, Quinn GF pretzels

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from wheat.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Glutino GF crackers, Simple Mills crackers, Mary's Gone Crackers

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat flour is the primary structural ingredient in commercial licorice candy.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: YumEarth GF licorice

confirmed_safe_products: Plain potato chips (Lay's Classic — confirm GF)

product_categories.category_warning: Pure chocolate is often safe but shared equipment and additives create risk. Shared equipment warnings are extremely common in this category.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat flour. See snacks category.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: YumEarth GF licorice

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Malted milk = barley malt + wheat. Core product contains gluten.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: None currently available

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wafer/biscuit layer contains wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Plain chocolate bars (check label)

confirmed_safe_products: Hershey's milk chocolate (no wheat ingredients — shared equipment note)
Reese's peanut butter cups (no wheat ingredients — shared equipment note)

product_categories.category_warning: Restaurant food carries cross-contamination risk even when ingredients are theoretically GF. This section covers dishes where the recipe itself commonly contains hidden gluten.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Virtually always made with soy sauce (wheat).
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Request with GF soy sauce or coconut aminos

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Imitation crab (surimi) contains wheat starch. Soy sauce alongside contains wheat.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Order sashimi, request GF soy sauce

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat flour roux is the thickening base in virtually all cream soups.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Clear broth soups, request ingredient list

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Cross-contamination from shared fryer oil with breaded items. Some fries are battered with wheat.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Ask for dedicated GF fryer

confirmed_safe_products: Chipotle bowl/salad (no tortilla, confirm fresh gloves)

product_categories.category_warning: Standard baked goods are all unsafe. GF bakery items from shared kitchens carry high cross-contamination risk. Only dedicated GF bakeries are reliable.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Canyon Bakehouse, Udi's, Little Northern Bakehouse

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from semolina (durum wheat).
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Barilla GF pasta, Tinkyada rice pasta

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Simple Mills, Mary's Gone Crackers, Glutino

product_categories.category_warning: Plain dairy is almost always safe. Flavoured varieties and some alternatives carry risk.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Made from oats with high cross-contamination risk. Only certified GF oat milk is acceptable.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Oatly GF variant, Planet Oat certified GF, almond/coconut/rice milk

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Contains barley malt and wheat flour.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Plain hot chocolate, cocoa powder

product_categories.category_warning: Most canned soups contain wheat flour or barley. Always check for explicit GF label.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat flour is the thickening base. Not gluten-free.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Pacific Foods GF cream soups

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Barley is gluten-containing.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Amy's GF soups, Progresso GF soups (selected varieties)

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat noodles plus soy sauce in flavour packet.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Lotus Foods GF ramen

confirmed_safe_products: Plain canned fish (tuna, salmon in water)

product_categories.category_warning: Fresh unprocessed meat is safe. Processed, marinated, self-basting, and seasoned meats carry risk.

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Self-basting solution often contains hydrolyzed wheat protein or modified starch.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Natural unflavoured turkey, baste yourself with GF ingredients

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Wheat starch binder used in all standard surimi products.
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Real crab meat

confirmed_unsafe_product_types.reason: Marinades typically contain soy sauce (wheat).
confirmed_unsafe_product_types.safe_alternative: Plain meat, marinate yourself with GF soy sauce or coconut aminos

confirmed_safe_products: Fresh unprocessed beef, chicken, pork, lamb, turkey, fish
Plain frozen meat (no marinade or seasoning)
Canned tuna in water or oil (plain)

high_risk_patterns.warning: Contains malt flavoring (barley). NOT gluten-free.
Most brands contain malt flavoring (barley). Check for explicit GF label.
Most contain non-certified oats or wheat. Check for certified GF label.
Contains malt — barley-derived. NOT gluten-free.
Traditional soy sauce contains wheat. Only GF-labelled soy sauce is safe.
Based on soy sauce — contains wheat unless explicitly GF-labelled.
Contains wheat flour. Not gluten-free.
UK/Canada versions contain malt vinegar. US Lea & Perrins is GF. Check country of origin.
Commercial licorice contains wheat flour. Not gluten-free unless explicitly labelled.
Contains wheat flour. Not gluten-free.
Contains wheat flour. Not gluten-free.
Malt-based centre. Not gluten-free.
Malt-based. Not gluten-free.
Gluten-removed beer — NOT considered safe for celiac disease. Residual gluten undetectable by standard tests.
NOT safe for celiac. 'Gluten removed' products retain residual gluten that standard ELISA tests cannot accurately measure.
NOT safe for celiac. Same issue as gluten-removed.
Imitation seafood — contains wheat starch binder. Not gluten-free.
Contains wheat starch. Not gluten-free.
Check for certified GF label — oats are high cross-contamination risk.
Standard Oatly is not certified GF. Only Oatly GF variant is acceptable for celiac.
Controversial for celiac. General Mills sorting process not universally accepted. GFWD advises caution.
Not certified gluten-free. High cross-contamination risk for celiac disease.
Wheat-based breadcrumbs. Not gluten-free.
Wheat-based. Not gluten-free unless explicitly GF-labelled.
Wheat noodles. Not gluten-free unless explicitly GF-labelled.
Wheat noodles. Not gluten-free.
Wheat flour thickened. Not gluten-free.
Wheat flour thickened. Not gluten-free.
